title: Files Workbench Plugin Manifest
description: manifest.json at the root of a plugin's source dir (/plugins/<id>/). Modelled on a Chrome extension manifest. A plugin declares a client and/or a server target.

manifest_version.description: Manifest format version. Only 1 is supported today.

icon.description: MDI path string or asset reference shown in the plugin UI.

client.description: Client (renderer) target. Built to a self-contained ES module loaded at runtime.

entry.description: Entry module relative to the plugin root, e.g. "client/plugin.js". Exports activate(api) and optional deactivate(api).

server.description: Sandboxed WASM backend target. The `entry` JS is compiled to WebAssembly and run on the Go server with no ambient authority beyond the granted permissions.

entry.description: Backend source module relative to the plugin root, e.g. "server/git.plugin.js".

runtime.description: Backend runtime. Only "wasm-js" is supported today.

permissions.description: Host functions the WASM backend may call. exec is parameterized per binary (exec:<name>).
Front-end capabilities. Each gates a slice of the WorkbenchAPI. Unknown values are ignored with a warning.

host_permissions.description: Host/backend access. Surfaced now; enforced once the backend bridge lands.

dependencies.description: Other plugins that must load first: { pluginId: semverRange }.

engines.description: Host compatibility, e.g. { "sdk": "^1.0.0" } — the @fw/sdk contract version.
